page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-16-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the c4d server family. It features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs and 62.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 3.88 GB of RAM per core. This configuration provides a balanced ratio of compute power to memory resources, aligning with general-purpose workload requirements.

The instance operates with dedicated CPU allocation, ensuring consistent processing performance without resource contention from other tenants. No graphics processing units are included in this configuration, and the local storage size is specified as None. Benchmark performance data is not available for this specific instance type, meaning performance characteristics must be evaluated based on its core hardware allocation.

This resource profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring a steady balance of compute and memory without the overhead of specialized hardware accelerators or local storage arrays. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, application servers, and moderate database workloads that utilize network-attached storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-16-lssd is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance within the c4d family. It is configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 62.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.88 GB of RAM per core. The instance features dedicated CPU allocation to ensure predictable compute performance, though it does not include GPU accelerators or local storage. Lacking benchmark data, its performance profile is defined by its balanced compute-to-memory ratio. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for standard enterprise workloads, making it suitable for web serving, application hosting, and general-purpose business applications that rely on network-attached storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-16-lssd is a general-purpose x86_64 server featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs, 62 GB RAM, and no local storage, designed for balanced enterprise workloads.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 general-purpose server configuration featuring sixteen dedicated virtual processors and sixty-two gigabytes of system memory.
